key_insight: Training turbulence closures inside a PINN makes them solver-agnostic and mesh-free, enabling fast discovery of generalizable models across bluff-body shapes.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to robust RANS closures that transfer across geometries and can be trained quickly on limited CFD or PIV data.
method: Impose the RANS residual through automatic differentiation in a physics-informed neural network.
Train four closure forms: tensor-basis stress models, a non-local kinetic-energy-transport model, a learned length-scale variant, and a direct Reynolds-force model.
Stabilize deployment with input-gradient smoothing and a Lipschitz constraint, then test in a finite-element solver under leave-one-shape-out validation.
evidence_signal: Across six 2D bluff-body wakes at Re = 10^4, all closures improved over steady SST k-ω, and the direct-force model achieved the best out-of-sample performance with about 8.5% LOSO drag error.
watch_item: Because the closure is trained without a coupled forward solve, final performance still depends on solver stability and careful deployment in the target CFD code.
paper_title: Generalizable turbulence closures across bluff-body shapes by PINN-based solver-agnostic training
